The Year of Mysteries.name: 谜团之年 The Year of Mysteries

Guiding Lights.name: 引导之光 Guiding Lights
Guiding Lights.text:
出自艾瑟里娅·尼克斯的档案
指引之光

当大多数人想到鬼火时，他们想到的是那些散发着恶意光辉的球体：它们排斥魔法、以恐惧为食，并将旅人引向注定的毁灭。然而，尽管符合这种描述的鬼火或许是在现代最常被遭遇到的一类，但它们既不是故事的开端，也不是结局。

首先必须考虑到，许多所谓的鬼火目击其实根本不是那么回事。夜里在沼泽上方看到的光，更可能是萤火虫、发光蘑菇、灯笼或由旅人携带的魔光，甚至只是些许燃烧的沼泽气体所致。又或者，它们可能是幽灵（特征）、闹鬼现象，或其他不安灵魂的显现。它们甚至可能是一些人编织出来的幻象，意在借助传奇牟利——不论是为了警告漫游者远离他们想保护的事物，还是为了把受害者引入他们的陷阱。

至于真正的鬼火，那些符合大众印象的最有可能显露自身，也最有可能恐吓社群。因而，它们也就最为著名，这也是自然之事。然而，环绕它们的光，以及具备采取球形形态的能力，才是作为一个物种的鬼火所共有的普遍特征。以恐惧为食与排斥魔法则是某个特定亚群的特征，被称为折磨者鬼火。折磨者鬼火可以由同类繁衍而生，但鬼火也能通过进行某种特定的仪式而成为折磨者鬼火。该仪式的确切细节迄今仍逃过我的掌握，但我已确定它需要大量灵魂作为燃料，并且只能在某些偏远地点施行。我怀疑这一仪式要么就是最初折磨者鬼火的直接起源，要么与其起源有着极其紧密的关联。我也已取得数个折磨者鬼火的遗骸以供研究。此事并不容易，因为它们的身体在死亡后往往会迅速消失，而它们的魔法抗力也使大多数常规手段无功而返。但正如所谓的“空的空间”与“虚无”并不相同，我深知在信息的缺席之处仍有许多可学。我研究了魔法如何从它们身上滑落、它们空无的形状。而我不禁注意到，其中回荡着“谜团”的手笔。

创造这一仪式的最初动机并不容易辨明，因为也许回答“为何谜团不想腐化一个物种中相当比例的成员”会比回答“为何他们会这样做”更容易。谜团是否像鬼火本身一样，正试图引导我们走向某物？抑或他们正试图迷惑我们，或吓得我们远离某个更深层的真相？

— 艾瑟里娅·尼克斯，空间元素的先驱，怪物代言人

From the Archives of Aitheria Nyx
Guiding Lights

When most people think of will-o’-wisps, they think of the glowing spheres of malevolence that repel magic, eat fear, and lure travelers to certain doom. But while wisps who fit that description may be the most commonly encountered in modern times, they aren’t the beginning or the end of the story.

It must first be considered that many of the supposed sightings of will-o’-wisps are in fact nothing of the sort. Lights seen out over the swamp at night are more likely to be fireflies, glowing mushrooms, lanterns or magelight carried by travelers, or even the products of a bit of burning swamp gas. Or they might be ghosts, hauntings, or other manifestations of unquiet souls. They might even be illusions spun by people looking to capitalize on legends, whether to warn wanderers away from something they wish to protect or to draw victims into their snare.

When it comes to true will-o’-wisps, those that fit with the common conception are the most likely to reveal themselves, as well as the most likely to terrorize communities. It’s only natural, then, that they are also the most famous. However, only the light that surrounds them and the ability to take spherical form are universal characteristics of will-o’-wisps as a species. Eating fear and repelling magic are characteristics of a specific subgroup, called tormentor wisps. Tormentor wisps can be born of others of their kind, but will-o’-wisps can also become tormentor wisps by undertaking a particular ritual. The ritual’s exact details have eluded me thus far, but I’ve determined that it requires numerous souls for fuel and can only be conducted in certain remote locations. I suspect that this ritual is either the direct origin of the first tormentor wisps, or else strongly connected to their origin. I’ve also procured the remains of several tormentor wisps for study. It hasn’t been easy, as their bodies have a tendency to vanish quickly upon death, and their magic resistance prevents most conventional methods. But much as that which is called empty space is distinct from nothingness, I know well that there is much to learn in the absence of information. I’ve studied the way that magic slides off of them, the shape of their emptiness. And I can’t help but notice echoes of the work of the Enigmas.

The initial motivations for creating the ritual aren’t simple to discern, as it is perhaps easier to answer the question of why the Enigmas wouldn’t want to corrupt a notable percentage of the members of a species than why they would. Like will-o’-wisps themselves, are the Enigmas trying to guide us to something? Or are they trying to confound or scare us away from a deeper truth?

— Aitheria Nyx, Herald of the Space Element, Advocate of Monsters

Will-O’-Wisp Ancestry.name: 鬼火 族裔 Will-O’-Wisp Ancestry
Will-O’-Wisp Ancestry.text:
鬼火是由空气与光构成的短暂存在。尽管它们最广为人知的形象，来自那些关于闹鬼的发光微尘把沼泽旅人引向溺毙结局的传说，但它们真正的动机却如夜空繁星般众多而多变。

鬼火投射到其周围环境的闪烁光芒，在照亮之余也以阴影掩藏了同样多的事物——而它们正合心意。毕竟，依照大多数鬼火所信奉的指引哲学，任何能够被完全理解的东西都几乎不值得注意，那它们又何必在上面浪费时间？同理，它们又为何要自贬身份，把自己呈现成一个其复杂之处能被轻易看透的“某个人”？

鬼火是自由不羁而神秘的存在，会以永不止息的热忱追逐复杂的目标。它们本能地明白，作为部分由秘法魔法驱动的存在，维持强烈的自我感与目的性对其持续存在至关重要。失去一切动力的鬼火有完全消散的风险，曾经鲜明的光芒会淡入雾中。然而即使在绝望里，它们也燃烧着不安分的能量，总在寻找意义的火花来重新点燃自身本质。若说有一条事实接近鬼火的普遍真理，那就是：它们憎恶被自己并非自愿承担的责任拴住。其他族裔的成员也许会把鬼火看作自私或懒惰——因为它们会不惜代价地避免背负不想要的期待之重。反过来，他们也可能钦佩鬼火追逐热情时所展现的决心与热忱。但对鬼火而言，这两种行为不过是维系生命的同一枚硬币的两面——是它们对个人自由坚定不移的奉献的映照。

鬼火鲜艳的光芒与毫不动摇的决心很容易引人注目。一旦吸引到注意，它们通常会利用这点去引导那些被迷住的人。它们也许会把受骗者引入各种陷阱与厄运之中，或哄骗他们做出有利于鬼火自身利益的行动。光谱的另一端，它们也可能扮演乐于助人的导师或守护天使，帮助受其照顾者达成目标——往往以看似隐晦或迂回的方式。对大多数鬼火而言，操纵是第二天性。它们乐于施为，也期待他人反过来试图操纵自己。它们觉得在一个充满对决诡计的世界里穿行，要比在一个规则森严、要求直白且几乎无法回避的世界里轻松愉快得多。

如果你想扮演一名拥有无法熄灭的内在光芒、带着神秘与玄妙气质的角色，你就应该扮演鬼火。

你可能会……

  • 享受走不寻常的路旅行。
  • 同样重视短暂体验与长久体验。
  • 将自己的过往与弱点保密，并更偏好与同样如此的人为伴，而不是与把心事写在脸上的人相处。

其他人很可能……

  • 觉得你可能是什么幽灵（特征）或灵体。
  • 觉得你迷人并想跟随你，看看你会把他们引向何处。
  • 假设你正试图戏弄他们。

折磨者鬼火

在许多并非鬼火者的认知里，这个族裔的名字几乎等同于“折磨者鬼火”——一个派系，其成员拥有相近的一套力量，并奉行一种以诱捕、恐吓与谋杀无数受害者为基础的相似生活方式。这类鬼火正是玩家可能习惯以怪物身份遭遇到的那种。它们以恐惧为食，并排斥几乎所有魔法。尽管它们比一般鬼火更强大，折磨者鬼火的知识与人格却彼此间诡异地相似。它们相信，不像它们的鬼火要么应被说服去执行噬魂仪式以转化为同类之一，要么就应被摧毁。

与其他鬼火相比，折磨者鬼火要小得多，其直径约为典型球形鬼火的一半。由于缺乏体力，它们也更难操纵物体。令其他鬼火恼火的是，即便旅人知道这一差异，他们也可能不愿冒险靠得足够近来确认眼前是否是折磨者。

生理描述

鬼火是由空气、光、元素魔法与秘法魔法构成的生物。尽管它们的明亮度可能让其看起来像幻象或幽灵（特征），它们的身体却具有物理形态与实体。大多数鬼火会在自身周围的灵光中散发光芒。它们能压制这层灵光来避免照亮其周围环境，使自己看起来更像全息影像而非灯塔。

在休息时，鬼火的身体会自然收缩成球形。以这种球形状态，它们可以在地面上滚动。它们会生成细小的触须以控制移动，抓握地面以停下，或蹬地以加速。它们也能维持最多两条大型触须，强壮且灵巧到足以携带并操纵工具，就像许多其他族裔的手臂与手一样。

多数喜欢与其他族裔互动的鬼火，会发展出一种次要形态，其外形类似于它们相处时间最长的那个族裔。次要形态通常包括手臂、用于说话的嘴以及其他可预期的外在装饰特征，也包括衣物与配饰。该形态会随它们一同成长，展现其年龄的外在迹象。除非经历影响其自我感的体验，否则鬼火次要形态的总体外形会保持一致；但它们能自由改变发型与服装等特征——这些都是被模仿族裔的成员也能轻易改变的。尽管如此，它们仍可穿戴其他衣物与配饰，只是除非能带来特定好处，否则很少有人这么做，因为若只是外观的一部分，它们能更轻松地改变装束。

鬼火也能自由改变其身体与发光灵光的颜色。有的偏好始终展示最爱的颜色；也有的会根据心情、季节或自身风格而改变。理论上，鬼火可以把自己呈现为统一的剪影，但它们几乎总会选择操控光芒的强度与色彩，以强调生理特征并营造一致的三维形象。

鬼火在球形状态下直径约2英尺，在次要形态下约3到4英尺高。就其体型而言，鬼火异常轻盈，通常重量在5到10磅之间。其高含气量使身体质地柔软如海绵；其体温取决于其元素魔法，而元素魔法也充当其光源。电鬼火比多数类人生物略冷，而火鬼火则往往明显更暖，不过接触时仍不足以造成灼伤。与此同时，被熄灭的鬼火其体温会迅速调整以匹配其环境。

鬼火冒险者

成为冒险者的鬼火会喜欢多种职业，拥抱探索的刺激与追寻被遗忘秘密的渴望。虽然它们远没有折磨者鬼火那般孱弱，但依然远比强壮更敏捷。武战鬼火通常偏好身手灵活、步伐轻快的近战职业，例如游荡者或游侠剑客，或偏好能让它们以精准与优雅从射程外进行打击的职业。具备魔法的鬼火最可能成为秘法施法者，例如吟游诗人、术士或女巫。它们也可能是某些冷门或神秘神祇的牧师。

社会

除去那些选择或生来就走上折磨者鬼火之路的鬼火，其他鬼火是个人主义者；它们描绘自己的道路时，依据的是令它们心生灵感之物，而非任何特定的社会期待。它们选择住在哪里、如何生活、与谁交往，以更好地追求目标。

尽管它们的道路可能导向截然不同的方向，但每个鬼火都从同一处起步。新的鬼火诞生于秘密会合：三名成体参与者会暂时融合为一道单一的璀璨光芒。当成体分离时，它们各自都会稍微变小，而一团新光会与它们相伴。幼年鬼火是脆弱的生物，需要父母定期灌注魔法以维持身体。它们从婴幼期成长的速度取决于父母魔法的鲜活程度。一旦鬼火能够自行移动、说话与觅食，父母往往就会避免给予直接协助或指示。相反，它们会为子女提供能让其按自身步调发现事物的环境。直到鬼火对世界有了足以自立的理解——包括形成驱动目标并具备追求它所需的技能——父母才会认为它们已成年。

尽管多数鬼火努力养育子女，持有苛刻人生哲学的鬼火则会把后代置于一连串严酷挑战的试炼之中。它们也许认为自己是在履行重要职责：教会子女从失败中重新振作，并避免被世界的幻象所蒙骗。

鬼火聚居地

鬼火传统上生活在沼泽与茂密林地中，那里雾气在古树间蜿蜒盘绕，幽幽灯火在死水潭上闪烁。废墟、破败墓园、深邃地穴以及其他能唤起神秘与失落之感的地方也同样受欢迎。当鬼火从零开始建造聚居地，而非选择本就合适的地点时，它们往往会修建曲折通道、暗门与房间，并以神秘莫测的物体收藏装饰其间，其用途对外人而言难以索解。柔和发光的符文或漂移的光点常用于标记边界或引导旅人——有时通向安全，有时通向险境。幻象与陷阱是常见要素，而其位置也并非总是固定。即便是长期居住在鬼火村落的居民，也应谨慎，不要过分相信自己的感官或记忆；因为村落本身仿佛在移动与呼吸，就像它也分享着鬼火对神秘的热爱。

微光

鬼火很少会把自己的一切都分享出去，即便对最亲近的朋友与盟友也是如此。它们倾向于独居或生活在因共同目标而聚在一起的小群体中。对鬼火而言，失去动力与目的可能关乎生死。因此，即使是最隐晦的一员，往往也会向细心的观察者透露是什么在驱动它——无论是它们有意告知，还是通过行动不经意地流露。这些驱动动机可以大致划分为若干类别，鬼火常称其为“微光”或“火花”。最常见的微光如下所述。单个鬼火可能属于多个微光，或在一生中在不同微光之间转变。

末日之光

“末日之光”一词指那些拥抱以恐吓并以杀戮他人为乐的生活方式的鬼火。许多末日之光是折磨者鬼火，但并非全部。非折磨者的末日之光有时被称为“哲学末日之光”。折磨者鬼火对大多数不与其共享生活方式者都很暴力，但对于足够强大的哲学末日之光——其可怖行径赢得了它们的尊敬——通常愿意破例相待。

哲学末日之光会因其经历而随时间变得更强。它们会依据各自天赋与偏好采取多样策略，例如用秘法魔法灌注恐惧、用手艺（技能）制造骇人的死亡陷阱，或用毒箭从远处缓慢狙杀受害者。它们会为了应对危险强敌而结成权宜的临时同盟，但除非有足够强大的领袖加以约束，否则很少有人能作为长期伙伴而被信任。

愚者之光

愚者之光是骗子与恶作剧者。它们喜欢颠覆预期，引诱他人陷入彻底的迷失与困惑。虽然几乎任何人都可能成为目标，但多数愚者之光更偏好用诡计揭露那些自以为强大者的弱点。心地良善的愚者之光可能会趁机打击傲慢之人，在其自负把自己拖入真正麻烦之前让他们收敛。中了它们把戏的后果也许令人尴尬，但不太可能造成长久伤害。光谱的另一端，残忍的愚者之光反而可能针对当地社区仰赖以自卫的英雄，选择会致残或致死受害者的诡计。究竟最恶意的愚者之光与末日之光之间界线何在，取决于不同解读。愚者之光通常偏好能赋予其幻术魔法、扭曲心智的法术、陷阱制作能力或其他多用工具来引人误入歧途的职业。

法师之光

法师之光将时间投入于研究魔法的奇迹。它们大多专注于秘法魔法令人眼花缭乱的谜题，但法师之光也可以是任何魔法传统的学者。它们可能喜欢发明新法术或重现失落魔法。实验性仪式、创新的法术塑形方式，以及其他在传统法术作业之外进行修补摆弄的手段，也都是常见研究主题。

法师之光往往觉得对魔法免疫的折磨者鬼火令人不快。这种不快可能源自恐惧，但同样可能来自一种感觉：折磨者鬼火以自己对魔法奇迹的无知为傲。它也可能来自一种广泛流传的怀疑——某位法师之光曾对制造最初折磨者鬼火的仪式负有责任。法师之光几乎总是属于施法职业。自发施法者倾向于专注理解在体内奔涌的力量之源与其本质；而预备施法者则更可能享受研究稀有法术。

悖论之光

悖论之光是哲学家。它们沉思令人困惑的谜题，并挑战他人同样去思考。它们可能偏好对情感本质的沉思、对历史事件数十种视角的深入研究，或其他带有矛盾特质的主题。若它们在表层找到了看似答案的东西，它们可能会寻找更深的洞见，或将注意力转向另一个问题。悖论之光寻求跨越众多族裔与文化的联系，以拓宽自身视角。它们也可能试验改变的意识状态，从不同角度看待事物。达到这些状态的策略包括深度冥想与催眠。

灵魂之光

灵魂之光会寻找迷失者，以帮助他们找到新的道路。它们相信，当一个人偏离为其设定的期待时，这意味着那个人的灵体与世界的要求之间存在不匹配。灵魂之光知道，没有两个人的处境完全相同。有时，开辟新道路需要帮助对方逃离压迫性的处境；另一些时候，则在于帮助对方拥抱潜能，而非逃避对未知的恐惧。但多数情况介于两者之间，需要鬼火既帮助创造机会，也促进内省。尽管灵魂之光可能十分乐于助人，它们并不会包办一切。多数灵魂之光认为把问题彻底一扫而空是傲慢、僭越且残酷的。毕竟那样做等于夺走它们想帮助之人的能动性，并剥夺其亲手将决心化为现实的机会。这并不代表所有接受灵魂之光帮助者都认同该观点。对大多数正接受灵魂之光援助的人而言，鬼火使用的工具往往是令人沮丧、恼怒与惊心的混合体。从隐晦线索与刺痛问题到艰巨试炼，灵魂之光从不以轻松著称。回顾自己所经历的事后，有些受其照顾者最终会对这些经历心怀感激——无论他们将其归因于这多管闲事的畸变怪，还是归因于妖精、神祇或单纯的命运心血来潮。

有些灵魂之光更偏好专注帮助活着之人改变人生，另一些则专注协助亡者。后者必须面对一个事实：亡者通常比生者有更为僵硬的执念。因此，这些灵魂之光并不总能保持微妙手段。多数情况下，协助亡者的灵魂之光的目标，是帮助他们的灵体前往来世。但对于那些保留了学习、成长与获得满足潜能的具智不死生物，灵魂之光可能会承担一种更类似于它们对活的生物所扮演的角色。

多数灵魂之光相信，引导迷失灵体是其族裔的原初目的，而末日之光则代表了对其真正感召的扭曲。面对并非折磨者鬼火的末日之光时，灵魂之光常会尝试说服其同族改变生活方式。但灵魂之光普遍认为折磨者鬼火已无可救药，最好在其暴力与腐化吞没脆弱社区之前迅速将其摧毁。

宝藏之光

宝藏之光痴迷于隐藏的宝藏。这些宝藏可能是历史遗物、珍贵宝石，或因鬼火自身私人理由而感兴趣的物品。若物体小到能携带，宝藏之光可能会将其收集起来并存放在安全之处。若是更大的物体，例如引人入胜的遗迹，宝藏之光则可能会定期踏上旅程去拜访自己的宝藏。

并非所有宝藏之光都执意保守宝藏的秘密。有些可能更愿意通过带领他人踏上旅程、揭开自己所发现之物来分享惊奇，但它们很少照亮直达的道路。它们对潜在寻宝者提出的诡计与试炼也许显得过度，但对宝藏之光而言，这都是乐趣的一部分。毕竟，轻易找到的宝藏，很可能也会被轻易视作理所当然。

漂泊之光

漂泊之光天生是探索者。它们乐于在广袤荒野地区漫游，或深入被遗忘的废墟。对某地了解得越少，那地对漂泊之光就越具吸引力。但只要能以新鲜目光、摆脱对可能发现之物的成见去造访，漂泊之光也能从已经绘制过地图的地点中获得乐趣。

无论是小群体旅行还是独自行动，漂泊之光通常会在第一时间看到可能伤害它们的其他生物时就逃离。不过，这种撤退并不总意味着鬼火不打算战斗。它们可能借此将追兵引入陷阱、不利地形或危险猛兽之中。当漂泊之光成为冒险者，尤其是与性格更为直来直去的盟友同行时，它们往往需要调整战术，以纳入应对正面冲突的方案。

信念

多数鬼火拒绝那些带有严格规则或宣称能提供简单答案的信仰体系。相反，它们更可能被那些倡导与其追求相契合理念的哲学与宗教所吸引。例如，幸运、旅行与冒险的神祇在宝藏之光与漂泊之光中很受欢迎；愚者之光可能崇敬诡计者神祇；末日之光则可能崇拜恐惧的神祇，甚至是邪魔。

无论其微光为何，鬼火都相信应尽量减少完全花在例行与机械任务，或花在不需多少努力就能解决的问题上的时间。当然，除最年幼者外的所有鬼火都明白生活需要大量苦差事，但它们会设法更快完成那些任务，或让心思忙于其他念头。

鬼火常被神秘情境与现象所吸引。它们很少追求对研究对象的定论式理解；相反，它们更偏好从多个角度捕捉洞见的一瞥。在传统鬼火哲学中，沉思悖论、矛盾与那些失落于时间长河的视角，与关注那些显而易见的细节同等重要，甚至更为重要。

随着时间推移，为避免陷入熟悉的生活节奏，大多数鬼火会被驱使走向越来越极端的行为。作为冒险者，这可能意味着寻找更危险的敌人狩猎或寻找更珍贵的宝藏。作为戏法师，这可能意味着对更强大或更敏锐的目标实施更精巧的恶作剧。作为学者，这可能意味着深入神秘而禁忌的档案库。理论上，鬼火可存活数个世纪，但很少有谁能长期承受其日益膨胀的野心所带来的后果。

常见典范 以点点闪光照亮诸多谜团，追逐滋养你光芒之物，尊重沉默与黑暗那不可测度的深邃

常见禁忌 停滞不前，允许自己相信已找到普遍真理，草率地追随他人的道路

姓名

传统的鬼火姓名冗长、曲折且满是元音。出于隐私与实用的考虑，多数鬼火会从全名中挑选1到3个音节作为昵称。它们也可能在不同语境中使用不同昵称，而每个昵称都带有其对应的身份。

示例姓名

Devil-Eater, Gatesmasher, Icebreaker, Krakenslayer, Aruthaniivurorialuoa (as Aru, Orial, and Lua), Cysaela, Eeloku, Hwasui, Lumina, Theovai.

鬼火美食

鬼火能吃下多数其他族裔能吃的食物类型，不过其过程与拥有常规消化系统的族裔所用方式不同。当鬼火触碰食物时，它们能将其颜色与营养吸收入体。食物的颜色会在鬼火形体中舞动。待它们用餐结束，留下的只是一堆灰色尘埃。

由于这种进食方式，鬼火体验味道的方式比多数其他族裔更依赖食物的颜色。尽管它们仍能尝到食材，颜色鲜艳或富含色彩的食物会有更大胆、更强烈的味道，而无色食物则味道更淡或更弱。

偏好不引人注意其进食外观的鬼火，可能会选择先把食物放入口中再将其化为尘埃，然后在餐后找个合适机会告退，把尘埃吐出。

有些鬼火会发展出以恐惧为食来维持自身的能力。这种力量是折磨者鬼火的知名能力，但并非它们所独有。

Will-o’-wisps are ephemeral beings of air and light. Though they are best known from tales of haunted glowing motes leading marshland travelers to a watery doom, their true motivations are as numerous and varied as the stars in the sky.

The flickering light that will-o’-wisps cast on their surroundings conceals just as much in shadow as it illuminates—and they wouldn’t have it any other way. After all, according to the guiding philosophy that most will-o’-wisps believe, anything that can be fully understood is hardly worth notice, so why would they waste time on it? And by the same token, why would they degrade themselves by appearing to be someone whose intricacies could be easily understood?

Will-o’-wisps are free-spirited and mysterious beings who pursue complex aims with unending fervor. They instinctively understand that, as beings powered in part by occult magic, maintaining a strong sense of self and purpose is vital to their continued existence. Will-o’-wisps who lose all motivation are at risk of dissipating entirely, their once-vivid lights fading into the mist. Yet even in despair, they burn with restless energy, always seeking a spark of meaning to rekindle their essence. If there is one fact that approaches a universal truth for will-o’-wisps, it’s that they resent being tied down by responsibilities they didn’t undertake willingly. Members of other ancestries might perceive will-o’-wisps as selfish or lazy for the lengths they’ll go to avoid being burdened by the weight of unwanted expectations. Alternatively, they might admire the determination and zeal with which will-o’-wisps pursue their passions. But to will-o’-wisps, these two behaviors are simply two sides of the same life-sustaining coin—a reflection of their unwavering devotion to personal freedom.

A will-o’-wisp’s vibrant lights and unflinching determination easily draw notice from others. Once they’ve captured attention, they typically use it to guide those they’ve entranced. They might lead their patsies into all manner of traps and misfortunes or trick them into actions that serve the will-o’-wisp’s interests. On the opposite end of the spectrum, they might act as helpful mentors or guardian angels who help their charges achieve their goals, often in ways that seem cryptic or indirect. Manipulation is second nature to most will-o’-wisps. They engage in it freely, and they expect others to attempt to manipulate them in turn. They find it far easier and more pleasant to maneuver in a world of dueling tricks than one with strict rules and direct demands that are nearly impossible to work around.

If you want to play a mystical and mysterious character with an unquenchable inner light, you should play a will-o’-wisp.

You Might...

  • Enjoy traveling off the beaten path.
  • Value fleeting experiences just as much as lasting ones.
  • Keep your history and weaknesses private, and prefer the company of others who do the same to those who wear their hearts on their sleeves.

Others Probably...

  • Think that you might be some sort of ghost or spirit.
  • Find you fascinating and want to follow you to see where you’ll lead them.
  • Assume that you’re trying to trick them.

Tormentor Wisps

In the minds of many who aren’t will-o’-wisps, the name of the ancestry is synonymous with tormentor wisps, a faction whose members have a similar set of powers and follow a similar lifestyle based on entrapping, terrorizing, and murdering countless victims. These are the sorts of will-o’-wisps that players might be used to encountering as monsters. They derive sustenance from fear and repel almost all magic. While they are stronger than the average will-o’-wisp, tormentor wisps’ knowledge and personalities are eerily similar to each other. They believe that will-o’-wisps who aren’t like them should either be convinced to perform soul-eating rituals to transform into another of their kind or else be destroyed.

When compared to other will-o’-wisps, tormentor wisps are considerably smaller, with a diameter around half that of a will-o’-wisp’s typical ball form. They also struggle to manipulate objects due to a lack of physical strength. Much to the chagrin of other will-o’-wisps, even travelers who are aware of this difference might not want to risk getting close enough to confirm if they’re looking at a tormentor.

Physical Description

Will-o’-wisps are beings formed of air, light, elemental magic, and occult magic. While their luminance might make them look like illusions or ghosts, their bodies have physical form and substance. Most will-o’-wisps exude light in an aura around them. They can suppress this aura to avoid illuminating their surroundings, making them look more like holograms than beacons.

When at rest, a will-o’-wisp’s body naturally contracts into a spherical form. In this ball shape, they can roll across the ground. They generate tiny tendrils to control their movement, gripping the ground to stop or pushing off the ground to accelerate. They can also maintain up to two large tendrils that are strong and dexterous enough to carry and manipulate tools, much like the arms and hands of many other ancestries.

Most will-o’-wisps who enjoy interacting with other ancestries develop a secondary form that is shaped like the ancestry they’ve spent the most time around. Secondary forms often include arms, a mouth for speech, and other external cosmetic features that would be expected, as well as clothing and accessories. This form grows with them, showing external signs of their age. While the general shape of a will-o’-wisp’s secondary form is consistent unless they undergo an experience that affects their sense of self, they can freely change features like hairstyle and clothing that members of the emulated ancestry could easily change. This doesn’t prevent them from wearing other clothing and accessories, though few choose to do so unless it provides a specific benefit, since they can change their outfit more easily if it’s just a part of their appearance.

Will-o’-wisps can also freely change the color of both their bodies and their glowing auras. Some prefer to display their favorite colors at all times, while others change to fit with their moods, the seasons, or their sense of style. While a will-o’-wisp could hypothetically present themselves as a uniform silhouette, they almost always choose to manipulate the intensity and color of their light to highlight physical features and create a consistent three-dimensional image.

Will-o’-wisps are about 2 feet in diameter in ball form and around 3 to 4 feet tall in their secondary form. Will-o’-wisps are exceptionally lightweight for their size, typically weighing between 5 and 10 pounds. Their high air content gives their bodies a soft and spongy texture, while their body temperatures depend on their elemental magic, which also serves as their light source. Electricity wisps are slightly colder than most humanoids, while fire wisps tend to be significantly warmer, though not quite enough to cause burns on contact. Meanwhile, an extinguished wisp’s body temperature rapidly adjusts to match their environment.

Will-o’-Wisp Adventurers

Will-o’-wisps that become adventurers enjoy a wide range of classes, embracing the thrill of exploration and the pursuit of forgotten secrets. While they aren’t nearly as weak as tormentor wisps, they are still far more dexterous than strong. Martial will-o’-wisps typically favor melee classes that are nimble and light on their feet, such as rogue or swashbuckler, or classes that let them strike from range with precision and grace. Will-o’-wisps with magic are most likely to be occult spellcasters, such as bards, sorcerers, or witches. They might also be clerics of obscure or mysterious deities.

Society

Outside of will-o’-wisps that choose or are born into the path of the tormentor wisp, will-o’-wisps are individualists who chart a path based upon what inspires them rather than any particular set of societal expectations. They choose where they live, how they live, and with whom they associate to pursue their goals better.

While their paths might lead them in wildly different directions, every will-o’-wisp starts in the same place. New will-o’-wisps are born from secretive meetings that involve three adult participants temporarily merging into a single vibrant light. When the adults separate, they are each slightly smaller, and a new light accompanies them. Baby will-o’-wisps are fragile creatures that require regular infusions of magic from their parents to maintain their bodies. The speed at which they mature out of infancy depends upon the vibrancy of their parents’ magic. Once will-o’-wisps can move, speak, and feed themselves, parents tend to refrain from giving them direct assistance or instruction. Instead, they present their children with environments that give them opportunities to discover things at their own pace. Will-o’-wisps aren’t considered to have reached adulthood by their parents until they’ve attained enough understanding of the world to support themselves, including developing a driving goal and the skills needed to pursue it.

While most will-o’-wisps endeavor to nurture their children, will-o’-wisps with harsh philosophies on life instead present their offspring with a grueling gauntlet of challenges. They might believe themselves to be fulfilling an important role of teaching their children to pick themselves back up from failures and avoid being taken in by the world’s illusions.

Will-o’-Wisp Settlements

Will-o’-wisps traditionally live in marshes and dense woodlands, where fog curls through ancient trees and ghostly lights shimmer across stagnant pools. Ruins, decrepit graveyards, cavernous catacombs, and other places that evoke feelings of mystery and loss are also popular choices. When will-o’-wisps build settlements from scratch rather than choosing locations already suitable, they tend to construct winding passageways, secret doors, and chambers adorned with esoteric collections of objects whose purpose is inscrutable to outsiders. Softly glowing runes or drifting motes of light often mark boundaries or guide travelers—though sometimes to safety, and sometimes to peril. Illusions and traps are common features, and their locations aren’t always static. Even long-term residents of a will-o’-wisp village should be careful not to trust their senses or their memories too much, for the village itself seems to shift and breathe, as if it shares in the will-o’-wisps’ love of mystery.

Glimmers

Will-o’-wisps are rarely the type to share everything about themselves, even with their closest friends and allies. They tend to live alone or in small groups brought together by shared goals. Losing motivation and purpose can be a matter of life or death for will-o’-wisps. As such, even the most cryptic of their numbers tend to reveal what drives them to a careful observer, whether they consciously tell others or let it slip through their actions. These driving motivations can be roughly divided into categories, which will-o’-wisps often call glimmers or sparks. The most common glimmers are detailed below. A single will-o’-wisp might belong to several glimmers or shift between glimmers over the course of its life.

Doom Lights

The term doom light refers to will-o’-wisps who embrace the lifestyle of terrifying and killing others for sport. Many doom lights are tormentor wisps, but not all. Non-tormentor doom lights are sometimes called philosophical doom lights. While tormentor wisps are violent with most others who don’t share their way of life, they’re usually willing to make an exception for sufficiently powerful philosophical doom lights whose terrible works have earned their respect.

Philosophical doom lights grow stronger over time as a result of their experiences. They use a variety of strategies based upon their individual talents and preferences, such as occult magic to instill fear, crafting skills to create horrific deathtraps, or poisoned arrows to slowly snipe down their victims from afar. They form temporary alliances of convenience to deal with dangerous foes, but few can be trusted as long-term companions unless they are kept in line by a sufficiently powerful leader.

Fool’s Lights

Fool’s lights are tricksters and pranksters. They enjoy subverting expectations and baiting others into becoming utterly lost and confused. Though just about anyone could become a target, most fool’s lights prefer to use their tricks to expose the weaknesses of those who believe themselves to be strong. Good-natured fool’s lights might take the opportunity to knock arrogant people down a peg before their egos get them in real trouble. The consequences of falling for their tricks might be embarrassing, but they’re unlikely to cause lasting harm. On the opposite side of the spectrum, cruel fool’s lights might instead target the heroes that local communities rely on for self-defense, choosing tricks that maim or kill their victims. Where exactly the line falls between the most malicious of fool’s lights and doom lights is a matter of interpretation. Fool’s lights usually prefer classes that grant them illusion magic, mind-bending spells, snare-crafting abilities, or other versatile tools for leading others astray.

Mage Lights

Mage lights devote their time to studying the marvels of magic. Most of them focus on the bewildering riddles of occult magic, but mage lights can be scholars of any magical tradition. They might enjoy inventing new spells or rediscovering lost magic. Experimental rituals, innovating forms of spellshaping, and other means of tinkering outside of traditional spellwork are also common subjects of study.

Mage lights often find the magic-immune tormentor wisps distasteful. This distaste could be born of fear, but is just as likely to come from a sense that tormentor wisps take pride in their ignorance of the wonders that magic can bring. It might also come from the commonly held suspicion that a mage light was responsible for the ritual that created the first tormentor wisps. Mage lights almost always belong to spellcasting classes. Spontaneous casters tend to focus on understanding the source and nature of the power that surges through them, while prepared casters are more likely to enjoy studying rare spells.

Paradox Lights

Paradox lights are philosophers. They ponder baffling riddles and challenge others to do the same. They might prefer contemplation on the nature of emotions, deep studies into dozens of perspectives on historical events, or other subjects with contradictory qualities. If they find what feels like an answer on the surface, they might search for deeper insights, or else change their attention to another problem entirely. Paradox lights seek connections across numerous ancestries and cultures to help broaden their perspectives. They might also experiment with altered states of consciousness to view matters from a different angle. Strategies for reaching these states include deep meditation and hypnosis.

Soul Lights

Soul lights seek out the lost, so that they might help them find a new path. They believe that when someone strays from the expectations set out for them, it’s a sign that there’s a mismatch between that person’s spirit and the world’s demands. Soul lights know that no two people’s sets of circumstances are the same. Sometimes, creating a new path requires helping the person escape an oppressive situation. In other cases, it’s all about helping the person embrace their potential instead of running away from their fear of the unknown. But most situations fall somewhere in between, requiring the will-o’-wisp to help both with creating opportunities and promoting introspection. As helpful as soul lights can be, they don’t do all the work. Most soul lights would consider fully sweeping a problem away to be arrogant, presumptuous, and cruel. After all, to do so would be to steal away the agency of the person they are trying to help, and to rob them of the chance to make their determination manifest with their own two hands. That’s not to say all of those who receive assistance from a soul light share this perspective. For most people who are currently receiving a soul light’s assistance, the tools that the will-o’-wisp uses are some blend of frustrating, infuriating, and harrowing. From cryptic clues and painful questions to daunting trials, soul lights aren’t known for making things easy. Looking back on what they’ve faced, some of a soul light’s charges wind up feeling grateful for their experiences, whether they attribute them to the meddlesome aberration or to other forces like fairies, the gods, or simply the whims of fortune.

Some soul lights prefer to focus on helping the living change their lives, while others focus on assisting the dead. Those in the latter category must contend with the fact that the dead usually have much more rigid attachments than the living. As such, these soul lights can’t always afford subtlety. In most cases, the goal of soul lights who assist the dead is to help their spirits pass on to the afterlife. But for sapient undead who retain the potential to learn, grow, and experience fulfillment, soul lights might take on a role more similar to the one they play for living creatures.

Most soul lights believe that guiding lost spirits was the original purpose of their ancestry, and that doom lights represent a perversion of their true calling. When faced with doom lights who aren’t tormentor wisps, soul lights often try to convince their kin to change their way of life. But soul lights widely regard tormentor wisps as lost causes, best destroyed quickly before their violence and corruption can overtake vulnerable communities.

Treasure Lights

Treasure lights are obsessed with hidden treasures. These treasures might be historical relics, valuable gems, or items that interest the wisp for their own personal reasons. If the objects are small enough to carry, treasure lights might gather them up and store them somewhere safe. For larger objects, like fascinating ruins, the treasure light might instead make regular journeys to visit their treasure.

Not all treasure lights are determined to keep their treasure secret. Some might prefer to share their wonder by leading others on a journey to uncover what they’ve found, but they rarely illuminate direct paths. The tricks and trials they pose to would-be treasure seekers might seem excessive, but to treasure lights, it’s all part of the fun. After all, a treasure easily found is liable to be easily taken for granted.

Wandering Lights

Wandering lights are explorers at heart. They delight in roaming vast wilderness regions or delving into forgotten ruins. The more that’s unknown about a location, the more enticing it is to wandering lights. But wandering lights can also find delight in visiting places that have already been charted, as long as they can do so with fresh eyes and free from preconceptions about what they might find.

Whether they travel in small groups or alone, wandering lights usually flee at the first sight of other creatures that could mean them harm. This retreat doesn’t always mean that the wisps don’t intend to fight, though. They might use it to lure pursuers into traps, disadvantageous terrain, or dangerous beasts. When wandering lights become adventurers, especially those who travel with allies of a more straightforward disposition, they often need to adapt their tactics to include responses to direct confrontations.

Beliefs

Most will-o’-wisps reject belief systems that come with firm rules or that claim to provide easy answers. Instead, they are more likely to be drawn to philosophies and religions that promote ideals that are compatible with their pursuits. For example, deities of luck, travel, and adventure are popular for treasure lights and wanderer lights, while fool’s lights might venerate trickster deities, and doom lights might worship deities of fear or even fiends.

Regardless of their glimmer, will-o’-wisps believe that it’s important to minimize the amount of time spent exclusively on routine and rote tasks or on problems that can be solved without much effort. Of course, all but the youngest will-o’-wisps understand that life requires plenty of drudgery, but they look for ways to get those tasks completed more quickly or to keep their minds occupied on other thoughts.

Will-o’-wisps are often drawn to mysterious situations and phenomena. They seldom seek to reach a definitive understanding of what they study; rather, they prefer to catch glimpses of insight from numerous angles. In traditional will-o’-wisp philosophy, it is just as important, if not more so, to contemplate paradoxes, contradictions, and perspectives that are lost to time than it is to focus on details that are readily apparent.

Over time, the desire to avoid sinking into a familiar rhythm of life drives most will-o’-wisps toward increasingly extreme behavior. As adventurers, this might mean seeking ever more dangerous foes to hunt or treasures to find. As tricksters, it might mean more elaborate pranks against more powerful or perceptive targets. And as scholars, it might mean delving into esoteric and forbidden archives. A will-o’-wisp can theoretically survive for several centuries, but few survive the consequences of their ever-growing ambitions for that long.

Popular Edicts shine flickers of light on many mysteries, pursue that which feeds your light, respect the unfathomable depths of both silence and darkness

Popular Anathemas Remain stagnant, allow yourself to believe that you’ve found a universal truth, heedlessly follow another’s path

Names

Traditional will-o’-wisp names are long, convoluted, and full of vowels. As a matter of both privacy and practicality, most will-o’-wisps choose between 1 and 3 syllables from their full name to serve as a nickname. They might use different nicknames in different contexts, each with their own associated identity.

Sample Names

Devil-Eater, Gatesmasher, Icebreaker, Krakenslayer, Aruthaniivurorialuoa (as Aru, Orial, and Lua), Cysaela, Eeloku, Hwasui, Lumina, Theovai.

Wispy Cuisine

Will-o’-wisps can eat most types of food that other ancestries do, though the process differs from the methods that ancestries with conventional digestive systems use. When a will-o’-wisp touches food, they can absorb its colors and nutrients into their body. The colors of the food dance through the will-o’-wisp’s form. By the time they’ve finished their meal, all that’s left behind is a pile of gray dust.

Because of this method of eating, will-o’-wisps experience flavor in a way that’s more dependent on the color of food than most other ancestries. While they can still taste the ingredients, brightly colored food or food saturated with color has a bolder, stronger taste, while colorless food has a blander or weaker taste.

Will-o’-wisps who prefer not to draw attention to the way it looks when they eat might choose to place the food inside their mouths before reducing it to dust, then find a convenient opportunity to excuse themselves to expel the dust after the meal is finished.

Some will-o’-wisps develop the ability to sustain themselves by eating fear. This power is a well-known ability of tormentor wisps, but it isn’t exclusive to them.

The Best Laid Plans.text:
出自艾瑟莉娅·妮克斯的档案
最周密的计划

将魔法作为一种媒介，用来融合那些在其他情况下无法产生生物学后代的族裔，这是一种有充分记录的现象，尤其常见于研究那些受到其他位面影响的个体以及拥有龙族传承者的起源时。当无法直接归因于已知的配对关系时，这些显现往往被归因于父母在受孕或出生之前吸收了神秘影响，或是遥远祖先的特性再度显现。

在描述那些不会经历妊娠、不会产卵、也不会以类似方式在受保护的空间中孵育幼体的族裔成员时，情况就更难以说明。蘑菇人便是如此——一种类真菌之民，他们通过培育孢子床来繁衍后代。在大多数蘑菇人文化中，这些孢子床会混合数名不同蘑菇人的孢子、其他真菌生物的孢子，以及大量旨在促进健康生长的物质。在由数名蘑菇人共同铺下、数以百万计的孢子床中，会诞生从数个到数十个孩子不等。因此，从生物学意义上而言，亲缘关系更像一幅织锦，而非一个明确的概念。

为什么蘑菇人会与其他真菌物种杂交？这并不难解释，因为蘑菇人并不讳言他们从中获得的益处——既包括生理上的优势，也包括来自其他真菌近亲的防护。但这一过程的起源又是什么？我的研究表明，这些起源可能远比人们对这种以友善的社区建设者而享有盛名的物种所预想的更加阴险。我在一片古老森林深处发现了一块土地，那里浸染着一种与“谜团”的影响强烈相关的腐化。这片区域保存着休眠的蘑菇人孢子，以及一种与令人闻风丧胆的寄生性铃行者相似物种的休眠孢子。尤其值得注意的是，这些蘑菇人孢子缺乏在现代蘑菇人血脉中常见的多真菌影响。这些孢子床是否孕育了最初的蘑菇人？抑或“谜团”正试图创造他们自己的蘑菇人血脉？若那项实验的任何后裔如今仍存活，我未见其踪迹。然而，就我所能辨别的情况而言，现代蘑菇人的发展是由蘑菇人培育者推动的，而非异界影响所致。但蘑菇人真的逃过了“谜团”的注视吗？若现代培育者发现了与“谜团”的远古孢子或与铃行者杂交的方法，他们很可能会让自己重新踏上一条危险的道路。

这一实验地点引发了令人不安的两难。将其揭露以供进一步研究，可能会给“谜团”一个重启其工作的绝佳机会；但若对任何人都不说，又会使该地点的长期保护成为不可能之事。它的位置应当托付给谁？也许我抵达此地本身，就已足以引发我所希望阻止的那些事件。

蘑菇与谜团最周密的计划，终究也会被时代束缚而破碎。

——艾瑟莉娅·妮克斯，空间元素的先驱，怪物的代言人

From the Archives of Aitheria Nyx
The Best Laid Plans

Magic as a medium for blending ancestries that would not otherwise be able to produce biological offspring is a well-documented phenomenon, especially in the study of the origins of individuals with influence from other planes and those with draconic heritage. When not directly attributable to a known coupling, these manifestations are often attributed to parents absorbing mystical influence prior to conception or birth, or to the re-emergence of features from distant ancestors.

Matters are more difficult to describe when it comes to members of ancestries who don't experience pregnancy, lay eggs, or use a similar method of incubating a child in a protected space. Such is the case for mushies, fungoid people who cultivate beds of spores to produce offspring. In most mushie cultures, these beds blend spores from several different mushies with spores from other fungus creatures, as well as numerous substances intended to promote healthy growth. From beds of millions of spores laid by several mushies together, somewhere between several and dozens of children arise. So parentage in a biological sense is more of a tapestry than a concrete concept.

Why do mushies hybridize with other fungal species? That's easy enough to address, since mushies aren't secretive about the benefits they gain, both in terms of physiological advantages and defense from their other fungal relatives. But what are the origins of the process? My research suggests that these origins may be far more sinister than one might expect for a species with mushies' strong reputation as friendly community builders. I've located a patch of land deep within an ancient forest steeped with a type of corruption strongly associated with the influence of Enigmas. This patch holds dormant mushie spores as well as dormant spores from a species similar to the much-feared parasitic bell walkers. Of particular note, the mushie spores lack the multifungal influence that is common among modern mushie lineages. Did these beds house the first mushies? Or were the Enigmas seeking to create their own mushie lineage? If any descendants of that experiment live today, I've seen no signs of them. As far as I've been able to discern, however, the development of modern mushies has been driven by mushie cultivators, not otherworldly influences. But have mushies truly escaped the eyes of the Enigmas? If modern cultivators discover means of hybridizing with the Enigma's ancient spores or with bell walkers, they may well set themselves back on a dangerous path.

The experimental site raises troubling quandaries. To expose it for further study risks giving the Enigmas a golden opportunity to restart their work. But to tell no one makes the site's long-term protection an impossible proposition. To whom should its location be trusted? Perhaps my own arrival at the place was already enough to set in motion the very events I would hope to prevent.

The best laid plans of mushrooms and mysteries are bound in time to break.

— Aitheria Nyx, Herald of the Space Element, Advocate of Monsters

Mushie Ancestry.name: 蘑菇人族裔 Mushie Ancestry
Mushie Ancestry.text:
Mushies are friendly fungus people cultivated from a mixture of spores, including those from other fungal species. Thanks to these methods, mushies display considerable diversity in both their physical appearance and their abilities.

Best known for being sociable, curious, and adaptable, the mushroom-featured mushies can thrive pretty much anywhere, from underground caverns to deep forests and from mountain peaks to windy valleys. They closely observe the habits of others for ideas, whether they are watching for tips on how to handle a particular challenge or searching for a new hobby. That’s not to say that they heedlessly copy whatever they see. Sometimes, the lessons they draw from others are cau-tionary tales, rather than examples to emulate. When mushies find some-thing interesting, they are often quick to share it with their friends and acquain-tances. Mushie informa-tion networks are robust, if not entirely accurate. First-hand accounts are often decent, but by the time a mushie’s reporting something they heard fifthhand, listeners had best avoid assuming they are getting anything more than the general vibe of the original subject matter.

A mushie’s body blends spores from numerous mushies as well as multiple members of other fungal species. The way that they view these other species is a deeply personal and individual experience. They might treat a patch of nonsapient fungi that contributed spores to their body as a revered ancestor. Or they might have complicated feelings about a dangerous fungal creature from which they are descended. For mushies, it’s not uncommon to experience the uncanny sensation of almost understanding their ancestors’ instincts, like a word on the tip of the tongue or a memory that remains always just out of reach. This sense of wisdom just beyond the conscious mind is part of what drives mushies to deepen their connections with others and share what they’ve discovered. Perhaps even if they can’t find their own answers, they can help someone else find theirs.

If you want to play an offbeat character with an unusual body and a curious spirit, you should play a mushie.

You Might...

  • Share freely the information you come across with anyone willing to listen.
  • Take great interest in cultural practices that you’ve never seen before.
  • Feel a sense of kinship with other fungi.

Others Probably...

  • Assume that you have a vast social circle.
  • Think you’re a bit of a gossip.
  • Mistake you for another fungal creature, such as a fungus leshy or the species associated with your heritage.

Mushie Adventurers

Mushies’ curiosity and gregarious nature make them well-suited to an adventuring lifestyle. Mushie spellcasters often have ties to occult or primal magic that they call upon either through their intuitive understanding of nature or their force of personality. Mushie bards, druids, and sorcerers are more common than mushie witches and wizards. Mushies are also drawn toward classes that focus on a bond with one or more supernatural creatures, such as eldamon trainers from Battlezoo Eldamon and summoners. Martial mushies tend toward class options that allow them to set up advantageous conditions for their allies, such as barbarians or ruffian rogues who make good use of Intimidation, scoundrel rogues or swashbucklers who use tricky feints, or fighters who use combat maneuvers.

Mushies often have backgrounds that describe their connections to nature or the ways they interact with their communities, such as cook, emissary, herbalist, merchant, nomad, pilgrim, scavenger, scout, or warrior.

Physical Description

Mushies are mushroom people with broad mushroom caps on their heads. Most mushies have two hands, a mouth, at least two legs, and at least two eyes. They also typically resemble one or more non-sapient mushroom species native to the region where they sprouted. Features beyond those broad descriptors depend on the mushie’s heritage.

Bulbous Mushie: The rounded bodies of bulbous mushies originate from their bulbalos ancestors. They have two yellow eyes that protrude from their faces on stalks and red caps that produce a sticky slime. They balance their large bellies with between two and five legs.

Draconic Mushie: Draconic mushies are related to toadstool dragons. While toadstool dragons have scaly violet bodies with patches of mushroom caps, draconic mushies have mushroom bodies with patches of violet scales. Those who actively cultivate their draconic aspects can eventually manifest other toadstool dragon features, such as claws or wings.

Monospore Mushie: Monospore mushies are primarily descended from other mushies, with minor influence from common fungi in their area. They often have bright white bodies and red caps speckled with white scales. A veil extends down from their gills. Much like hair, this veil grows continuously and can be cut without causing harm, so monospore mushies often cut and shape their veils to suit their personal style. They have two dark eyes, often with pops of color along the edges, such as blue, green, or gold. Their wide mouths are flanked by a pair of pink-pigmented circles that mimic blushing cheeks.

Percussive Mushie: Percussive mushies are descended from drumcaps. Their bodies are typically squat and earth-toned. Their mushroom caps are tall and voluminous, with plenty of hollow resonating space. As they grow, their caps sprout numerous additional mushroom projections. Taken together, this set of mushrooms makes for an excellent home-grown drum kit. Percussive mushie hands are hard and rounded, with a single opposable thumb each. These thumbs are particularly flexible and nimble, allowing the mushie to grasp onto and manipulate objects freely despite the lack of other fingers. Percussive mushies typically have large mouths, which they protect with a flexible covering that extends down from between their two amber eyes.

Phantasmal Mushie: Phantasmal mushies are connected to phasmogorgia, a fungus that ensnares and devours souls. Their caps are covered in pustule-like growths that look like drops of blood. Faint blue wisps of spiritual energy sometimes gather about them, giving them an eerie glow.

Pseudospore Mushie: The features of pseudospore mushies are as varied as the non-fungal ancestries that they descend from. They might have patches of hair, fur, or scales. They might also have vestigial features like horns, tails, or snouts.

Thicket Mushie: Thicket mushies are prickly mushrooms, with spiny teeth and caps covered in green spines. They are the largest of mushies, inheriting a fraction of their thicket urchin ancestor’s towering size to reach an average height of around 7 feet.

Treesighted Mushie: Treesighted mushies have additional visual organs derived from their adaptation to coexist with the normally parasitic tree of eyes fungus. These organs are eye-shaped. Depending on the mushie, they might appear similar to the mushie’s other eyes, be large round eyeballs in a variety of colors, or have a more subtle form that looks more like a knot in a tree or even a tattoo than an eyeball.

Society

Mushie family structures are typically based around clusters, groups of mushies whose spores were planted around the same time and were cultivated by members of their community. Since there’s no telling which spores wound up being part of which mushie, mushies don’t have a concept of individual parenthood. Instead, all adults who participated in the cultivation are expected to contribute to child-rearing. Small clusters might have as few as 3 or 4 members, while large clusters contain dozens of mushies. While clusters share the same heritage, mushie communities might contain several different heritages. Multi-heritage communities are more common in areas where mushies have access to a variety of spores. These communities might even include polyspore mushies, who exhibit the features of multiple heritages. While these blended features are often cosmetic, particularly strong polyspore mushies sometimes develop advantageous abilities from multiple ancestral lines. Meanwhile, single-heritage communities predominate when aligning the community with a particular type of fungus, as it is advantageous for their survival.

Bulbous Mushie: Bulbous mushies significantly outnumber bolbaloses, due to their easier time reproducing, combined with their propensity for slightly more common sense than their naive ancestors. Still, bulbous mushies tend to be especially friendly and open-minded, to the point of being a bit too trusting for their own good. Bulbous mushies often hail from a settlement near a bolbalos, who is delighted by the mushies’ existence. Bolbaloses have a tendency to dote on mushies like children. When a bolbalos’s overly trusting nature gets them into trouble, though, they appreciate the work that the local mushies do to keep them safe, even if they do occasionally chide their mushie friends for being too harsh and cynical in their judgments.

Draconic Mushie: Most mushie heritages aren’t particularly picky about food, but draconic mushies share the rich sense of taste of their toadstool dragon forbears. As a result, food culture thrives in draconic mushie society, and fusion cuisine is particularly popular. While some of the types of nourishment that these mushies enjoy might be too rotten for most other ancestries to stomach, they pride themselves on the ability to produce delicious food to suit any palate. Draconic mushie communities are particularly close-knit and deeply intertwined with toadstool dragon colonies. Yet circumstances sometimes jolt them from these comfortable lives, whether they be external threats or an internal desire to explore what else the world has to offer. Draconic mushies are particularly likely to leave home alongside aeternal toadstool dragons.

Percussive Mushie: Percussive mushies tend to be loud and expressive communicators. Many percussive mushies are fluent speakers of Drumcap, the native tongue of their drumcap ancestors. By using that rhythmic language together with lyrics in another language, they produce songs and poetry with intertwining layers of meaning. They traditionally favor music as a means to share their history and commemorate important events over the written word. That the music changes over time with each generation’s interpretation is of little concern; in fact, most percussive mushies would consider these successive remixes to be adding new layers that better express the echoes of the past than a single dry set of scratchings on parchment.

Monospore Mushie: Monospore mushies are born without the influence of another powerful fungal creature, and the reason they were cultivated that way is often a key feature of their society. Some monospore mushies are a part of communities that once had a source of spores that is now lost. These communities might become nomadic in search of a new source, but they might also stay where they are to exact vengeance on whoever slew their ancestral guardian. Other monospore mushies belong to communities that remain monospore by choice. They might simply prefer monospore cultivation, or they might believe that monospore parents have a better chance of cultivating new potential mushie varieties for their offspring.

Phantasmal Mushie: Phantasmal mushies are connected to soul-eating phasmogorgia. Their common habits of speaking in hushed tones and staring off into the distance at wispy forms that other ancestries can’t see do little to dispel their spooky reputation. Yet phantasmal mushies are more likely to be caretakers of souls than to share their ancestors’ predatory habits. They often prefer careful reflection and meditation to quick action. They tend to settle in places thick with spiritual essence, such as sacred sites or locations where many souls have perished. Curiously, even though phasmogorgias’ ravenous hungers make them almost universally violent toward anyone who could potentially interfere with their meals, phasmogorgias seldom attack phantasmal mushies unless necessary for their survival. It’s been theorized that they don’t enjoy the taste of phantasmal mushie souls.

Pseudospore Mushie: Pseudospore mushies often prefer to live in communities alongside people from whichever ancestry is associated with their pseudospores. Unfortunately, while fungus creatures typically recognize mushies that share their spores as some sort of relative, the same does not hold true for non-fungal ancestries. Instead, the reception they receive is based more on cultural norms and the personalities of the individuals they come across. As such, while pseudospore mushies might find acceptance and belonging, they might also face suspicion or discrimination.

Thicket Mushie: Thicket mushies are related to thicket urchins, towering mindless fungi large enough to be mistaken for grassy hills. Despite the inherent danger thicket urchins represent to most other creatures, thicket mushies are safe from the giant’s predations. Many thicket mushie communities also develop means of steering the urchin, guiding it away from others they wish to protect and toward creatures that would threaten their homes. Thicket mushies have a long-standing rivalry with pukwudgies, who enjoy directing thicket urchins to raid settlements.

Treesighted Mushie: Treesighted mushies have reached a balanced symbiosis between the parasitic tree of eyes fungus and mushie fungus. Most of them are directly cultivated hybrids, but some treesighted mushies originate from mushies of other heritages who are infested with tree of eyes. Treesighted mushies have a reputation as meddlers who are always getting themselves involved in other people’s business. In their own communities, concepts like privacy and personal ownership tend to be fairly nebulous.

Mushie Settlements

of locations, especially in temperate or tropical climates. Mushie settlements are often densely packed, community-oriented, and welcoming to visitors of all ancestries. Mushies looking to found a settlement usually prefer to build near existing population centers, such as on the outskirts of town or in a cavernous undercity. Remote settlements are typically designed for a specific purpose, such as hubs for fungal research, waystations for travelers, or outposts to meet with reclusive fey. Wherever they are located, mushie settlements prioritize strong communication networks with other settlements. Few in mushie society are as highly regarded as those who carry letters and messages to maintain this vital infrastructure.

Mycelia

Most mushies value interconnectivity and community. Beyond the cluster in which they are born, they view themselves as related to all other mushies and many other fungal creatures besides. When classifying their social groupings, mushies consider the societal roles they hold. By sharing knowledge with and assisting others who fulfill similar roles, they can reach far greater heights. In honor of these connections, mushies often call their social groupings mycelia, named after the vast networks of rootlike structures that fungi use to connect, collaborate, and communicate.

Ancestor Speakers

Ancestor speakers help maintain good relationships between mushies and nearby fungal creatures, especially those whose spores are part of the local mushies’ cultivation strategies. Depending on the nature of both the mushie community and their fungal neighbors, this relationship might involve communicating with, learning from, worshiping, placating, or avoiding the fungi. Prominent ancestor speakers often learn to cast speak with plants to communicate with nonsapient fungi. While powerful sapient or ambulatory fungi receive the most attention, ancestor speakers also pay their respects to mindless, stationary fungi. By carefully observing the health of local fungus communities, they can find early warning signs of diseases or climate conditions that could eventually threaten mushies too.

Bone Scholars

Bone scholars take their name from the strange structural features that many animals and ancestries that resemble animals use to assist with mobility and protect vulnerable organs. They often keep collections of interesting bones that they find. Though these collections might seem menacing to bone-having creatures, mushies see little difference between wearing a bone necklace or a flower crown.

While this collecting habit gives the bone scholar mycelium its name, bone scholars don’t exclusively study bones. Bones just happen to be a convenient way to compare two creatures or to understand creatures that have since passed away. Instead, bone scholars grapple with philosophical questions about the nature of concepts like having an ancestral identity and what separates one type of creature from another. For example, many bone scholars are fascinated by the sheer number of humanoid ancestries that to their eyes look almost identical, such as humans, halflings, dwarves, orcs, and elves. Other bone scholars might ponder over questions like why there are so many different sorts of creatures that all figured out how to fly despite having otherwise dissimilar bodies, like bats, birds, and insects.

Cultivators

Cultivators search the world for new types of fungi, with a particular focus on species that might be compatible with mushies. While the most flashy cultivators focus on fungi that are powerful enough to potentially serve as the basis for a new mushie heritage, many cultivators also take an interest in fungi that are compatible with multiple heritages. For example, cultivators who have recently moved to a new region might seek out fungi with natural defenses against that region’s pests or that do better in that region’s climate.

Emissaries

Emissaries are bridges between communities. They might be messengers who carry news from place to place or diplomats who build connections between groups that were previously unknown to each other. While bone scholars take an interest in the biology of other ancestries, emissaries are curious about cultures. They enjoy picking up on new traditions to bring back to their communities. The enthusiasm of emissaries has a tendency to outstrip their understanding, however, so not all of these traditions are even recognizable to members of their originating culture by the time the emissaries are done with them.

Sturdy Stalks

Sturdy stalks are mushies who prioritize serving as pillars within their community, rather than venturing out into the wider world. They hold essential roles, including laborers, artisans, farmers, medics, and leaders. Sturdy stalks can be of any age, but their average age is somewhat older than the other mycelia, as mushies who go on adventures in their youth often enjoy settling down to share their experiences as elders.

Though sturdy stalks might prefer to stay close to home, reality isn’t always so considerate. When they do become adventurers, they make for steady and reliable allies. They’re the most likely of all mycelia to wind up taking on the role of "team parent."

Beliefs

Mushies are attuned to the connections that tie together all creatures that live, as well as the inanimate elements that they rely upon. They often worship nature deities or primal spirits such as the Eld on Alacar. The most commonly venerated spirit among mushies is the Eternal Mycelia, a manifestation of the collective experiences and knowledge of all fungi. Adherents of Mycelianism believe that when they die, a portion of their soul will merge into the spirit’s form.

Popular Edicts seek new experiences, share what you learn, listen to unfamiliar perspectives

Popular Anathemas banish someone from a community unless their actions pose a danger to the safety of others, hoard resources without using them, repeat harmful actions for the sake of tradition

Names

Mushie communities usually name all members of a cluster at the same time and choose intentionally similar names for the group. The commonality becomes an identifier for the cluster within the community, which mushies might use in places where other ancestries would use a surname. As an alternative to surnames, mushies might choose a cryptic title that refers to a fond memory. While asking a mushie about the origin of their title is a proper part of polite introductions.

Sample Names

Amurys the Twice-First, Flirin Flin, Flovturin the Floating One, Gillfort, Myvenal Myal, Yulowop the Heapiest.

Mushie Spores

Mushies have developed specialized spores for a variety of functions. In addition to the traditional reproductive spores, they create signaling spores for nonverbal communication of emotions. While other fungus-based creatures like fungus leshies can learn to decode the meaning of signaling spores, they go unnoticed to members of ancestries without the appropriate receptors. Mushies who live among other ancestries quickly learn that their spore signals aren’t a universal method of communication. To compensate for these lost signals, mushies tend to exaggerate other nonverbal cues, such as body language or tone of voice, or even provide detailed verbal explanations of their emotions. With practice, mushies learn to gauge how much their non-mushie conversation partners can read without these aids.

Many mushies also produce defensive spores to poison or addle the minds of their foes. The properties of these defensive spores depend upon the other fungal species that have influenced the mushie’s heritage.
